meta.setting: windy state-line reach where wagon road, ferry mud, and law papers meet
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in border-country law and road pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Road movement, settlement, and rural pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Courthouse and law-paper texture for Northern California mining settlements.

image_prompt.subject: a leaning border post at a dusty road fork, a ferry landing below, deputies comparing folded papers, and a freight wagon waiting with nervous riders
image_prompt.scene: windy late afternoon; mud clings to the ferry road, one man watches from the brush line, papers are visible but unreadable
image_prompt.composition: square border-road scene with the leaning post in foreground, road fork splitting behind it, ferry mud and wagon pressure visible
image_prompt.camera: third-person road approach view, close enough to read paper handling and wheel ruts but not text
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the line feel real because people slow down at it, not because the post is grand.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine border-road scene
render_traits: slightly simplified deputies, riders, wagon, border post, road fork, and ferry landing
modest texture resolution on wood, paper, wax, mud, rope, cloth, leather, and dust
hard ambient occlusion under wagon wheels, post, boots, ferry rope, and road brush
windy late-day light with dust and river haze
believable 1899 law papers, coats, tack, wagon gear, and road objects
playable jurisdiction scene with visible route choice and social pressure
style_modifiers.palette: dust ochre, cedar gray, paper cream, wax dull red, river mud brown, coat black, rope tan, and pale windy sky.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable border names, paper text, signs, labels, or markings.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The border post, road fork, ferry mud, deputies, papers, wagon, and watching figure are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to 1899 road, freight, and law pressure.
The image supports jurisdiction, fugitive, route-choice, freight-paper, and rumor scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
